title: The Pilot's Logbook
subtitle: A curated collection of flight simulator experiences

journal.freeform:
# Captain's Notes

Welcome to your logbook. This is your freeform notes area — anything goes. Trip plans, aircraft observations, half-formed ideas, weather you remember, whatever.

The **Entries** tab next door is for dated journal entries if you'd rather log things that way.

Markdown is supported. Click anywhere in this text to edit.

missions.title: The Denali Job
missions.subtitle: Talkeetna → Summit Attempt → Talkeetna
missions.description: Position to PATK (Talkeetna), the real-life base for Denali flightseeing operators. Take off, climb hard, head north into the Alaska Range. Circumnavigate the summit (20,310 ft — most piston bush planes will be at their absolute service ceiling and gasping). Find a glacier shelf to ski-land on (Ruth Glacier amphitheater is the classic). Take off again. Return Talkeetna.
missions.brief: Reality check on altitude: a normally-aspirated bush plane will be making a fraction of sea-level power up there. Plan a slow, patient climb. If the sim refuses, accept a high-altitude pass at 14,000 instead and call it a moral summit. The PC-6 Porter can actually summit; the Norden cannot.

missions.title: Find Your Quiet Lake
missions.subtitle: Pick a forested lake. Land. Drift.
missions.aircraft: Grumman G-21A Goose, ICON A5, or DHC-2 Beaver on floats
missions.description: Pick a forested reservoir or lake somewhere meaningful to you — childhood camp, a place you went hiking once, a lake you've never visited but always meant to. Rent or position a float plane nearby. Splash down in a quiet cove. Cut engines, drift, listen to the water. Imagine you're 21 again. Climb out and return at golden hour.
missions.brief: If you don't have an obvious lake in mind: Lake Tahoe (CA/NV), Lake of the Woods (Ontario), Lake Powell (UT/AZ), or any Canadian Shield lake will all work beautifully. The Goose is the prettier choice; the A5 is the broke-college-kid version of this same flight.

missions.title: Grand Canyon East-to-West
missions.subtitle: Special Flight Rules Area corridor flight
missions.aircraft: Bonanza, NXCub, or anything with good visibility
missions.description: Start KGCN (Grand Canyon Airport). Fly the SFRA corridors east toward the confluence with the Little Colorado, then turn west all the way to Toroweap. Land back at KGCN. MSFS has the canyon as photogrammetry plus terrain mesh — this is one of the best sightseeing flights in the sim.

missions.title: The Matterhorn Lap
missions.subtitle: Sion to Zermatt to Sion
missions.description: Depart LSGS (Sion). Climb to 15,000 ft. Circle the Matterhorn pyramid. Continue to Zermatt heliport area for the views, then return. The sim's photogrammetry on this peak is excellent.

missions.title: Mount Everest Pass · Lukla to Lukla
missions.subtitle: World's most dangerous airport
missions.description: VNLK (Lukla) is the 'world's most dangerous airport' runway — short, sloped, surrounded by 20,000 ft peaks. Take off from there, fly the Khumbu valley, get visual on Everest, return to Lukla and land uphill. There is no go-around at Lukla.
missions.brief: Asobo did Lukla properly in MSFS 2024. The runway gradient and surrounding terrain are accurate enough to make this genuinely tense.

missions.title: Mount Cook · NZ Southern Alps
missions.subtitle: Glacier landings in Aoraki National Park
missions.description: NZQN (Queenstown) up to Mt Cook Aerodrome (NZMC). Fly Milford Sound on the way if you take the long route west. Glacier landing on Tasman Glacier. Asobo's New Zealand World Update made this region absurdly good.

missions.title: Milford Sound Approach
missions.subtitle: One of the great VFR approaches
missions.description: NZQN (Queenstown) west through the Southern Alps. NZMF (Milford Sound) sits at the head of a fjord with vertical 4,000-ft walls. The approach corridor is famous and unforgiving. Real NZ scenic operators do exactly this flight.

missions.title: Mount Fuji · The Lakes Lap
missions.subtitle: Tokyo to Fuji and back
missions.description: Depart RJTT (Haneda) and head west. Loop Fuji at 13,000 ft. Drop down through the Fuji Five Lakes on the north face. Return to Tokyo.

missions.title: Iguazu Falls · Border Run
missions.subtitle: Argentina/Brazil/Paraguay tri-border
missions.description: SARI (Iguazu, Argentina) or SBFI (Foz do Iguaçu, Brazil). Low pass over Devil's Throat. Fly the U-shape of the falls. The mist column is genuinely modeled.

missions.title: Niagara Falls Border Loop
missions.subtitle: US/Canada border crossing
missions.description: KIAG (Niagara Falls Intl) up the Niagara River. Loop Horseshoe Falls. Hop across the border airspace. Land KBUF or return.

missions.title: Norway Fjords · Bergen to Geiranger
missions.subtitle: Sognefjord and Geirangerfjord
missions.aircraft: PC-6 Porter or Beaver on floats
missions.description: ENBR (Bergen) north, weave through Sognefjord and Geirangerfjord. Land at ENAL (Ålesund) or splash down a fjord if floats. The MSFS Nordics update made this region a showcase.

missions.title: Iceland · Vík to Vatnajökull Glacier
missions.subtitle: Black sand, ice, lava
missions.description: BIRK (Reykjavík) east along the south coast. Vík beach, Skógafoss visible from the air, Jökulsárlón ice lagoon, then the white wall of Vatnajökull. Possible landing at BIHN (Hornafjörður).

missions.title: Patagonia · Torres del Paine
missions.subtitle: The three towers
missions.description: SCNT (Punta Arenas) north into the Paine massif. Loop the three towers. Continue to El Calafate (SAWC) over the Argentine border. The Andes here are stunning and underflown by most simmers.

missions.title: Salar de Uyuni · The World's Largest Mirror
missions.subtitle: Bolivia salt flats
missions.description: SLUY (Uyuni) flightseeing. The salt flat reflection during rainy season in the sim's weather is genuinely surreal. Fly low. Take photos. Return.

missions.title: Machu Picchu · Sacred Valley Approach
missions.subtitle: High-altitude Cuzco departure
missions.description: SPZO (Cuzco, 10,860 ft elevation — high DA from the wheels-up moment) northwest along the Urubamba River. Find Machu Picchu on its ridge. Loop Huayna Picchu peak. Return Cuzco.
missions.brief: The terrain mesh handles this well even without freeware POI mods.

missions.title: Saba · World's Shortest Commercial Runway
missions.subtitle: 1,300 ft with cliffs on both ends
missions.aircraft: Twin Otter (the only legal type IRL)
missions.description: TNCM (Sint Maarten) to TNCS (Saba). The runway is shorter than your high school football field's length. Approach is over the ocean and ends at a sheer drop. Iconic. Land it. Take off again. Try not to overshoot into the sea.

missions.title: Courchevel Altiport · Sloped Runway
missions.subtitle: 18.6% slope, no go-around
missions.description: LFLJ. 18.6% slope. You land uphill, take off downhill, no go-around. French Alps showpiece field.

missions.title: Sydney Harbor · Skyline & Bridge
missions.subtitle: Opera House, Harbour Bridge, Bondi
missions.description: YSSY (Sydney Kingsford Smith). Climb out, fly the harbor at 1,500 ft. Opera House, Harbour Bridge from above. Bondi Beach on the way back.

missions.title: Rio de Janeiro · Sugarloaf & Christ the Redeemer
missions.subtitle: Santos Dumont departure
missions.description: SBRJ (Santos Dumont, in the heart of downtown) is one of the most photogenic departure airports on earth. Climb out, swing past Sugarloaf, around Corcovado and Christ the Redeemer, low over Copacabana. Land back at SBRJ.

missions.title: Tokyo Bay Skyline
missions.subtitle: Haneda departure, all the towers
missions.description: Depart RJTT runway 16R, immediate left turn, fly the bay. Tokyo Skytree, Tokyo Tower, the Imperial Palace, Odaiba, Rainbow Bridge. Return RJTT or land RJTI (heliport).

missions.title: PNG Highland Bush Trip · Mt Hagen Sector
missions.subtitle: Sloped runways, missionary cargo
missions.description: AYMH (Mt Hagen) into the eastern highlands. Fly to strips like Gema (AYGM, sloped 1,638 ft runway at 5,070 ft elevation), Wuyabo (AYBY at 6,387 ft), and Ande (AYAD). These are real missionary/cargo strips with significant gradients. PNG is to bush flying what the Alps are to mountain flying.
missions.brief: If you install the freeware Orbx packs, you'll get genuinely modeled sloped runways. Highly recommended given the time you'll spend here.

missions.title: Alaska Bush Run · Anchorage to Kotzebue
missions.subtitle: Real bush mail/cargo route
missions.description: PANC → PAMC (McGrath) → PAUN (Unalakleet) → PAOM (Nome) → PAOT (Kotzebue). Real bush mail/cargo route. The MSFS Alaska handcrafted update made these strips genuinely interesting.

missions.title: Reno Air Races · Pylon Practice
missions.subtitle: P-51 Mustang on the deck
missions.description: MSFS 2024 ships the Reno Air Race courses (and the new Roswell ones). Burn your competitive impulse on this when you don't want narrative. The Mustang at 50 ft AGL around pylons is its own reward.

missions.title: Chernobyl Exclusion Zone Recon
missions.subtitle: Don't go below 500 AGL
missions.description: UKKK (Kyiv Zhuliany) north into the Exclusion Zone. Overfly Pripyat (the abandoned city is in the photogrammetry). The Sarcophagus is recognizable from altitude. Loop, return UKKK. The Optica's bubble canopy is perfect for this kind of slow observation.

missions.title: Bagan Temples · Myanmar Sunrise
missions.subtitle: 2,000+ temples in a hot air balloon
missions.description: VYBG (Bagan). Hot air balloon over the temple plain at sunrise. MSFS 2024 ships an actual hot air balloon. There are over 2,000 temples in the photogrammetry here.

missions.title: Victoria Falls · Border Run
missions.subtitle: Zambezi crashes into a mile-wide chasm
missions.description: FVFA (Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe) or FLLI (Livingstone, Zambia). Loop the falls themselves — the Zambezi pours into a mile-wide chasm with permanent mist. The scale is hard to fake.
missions.brief: The MSFS terrain mesh handles this well.

missions.title: Namib Desert · Sossusvlei Dunes
missions.subtitle: Red dunes, white pans
missions.description: FYWB (Walvis Bay) or FYSW (Swakopmund) inland to overfly Sossusvlei — red sand dunes meeting white salt pans, some of the tallest dunes in the world. Continue to Sesriem strip if you want to land. Pure Mars-on-Earth photography.
